title: Allocation and deallocation deterministic evidence bank

publication.changeSummary: Added digest-pinned deterministic executable evidence for Allocation and deallocation; AI-assisted draft remains human-unreviewed and exam-ineligible.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted deterministic draft awaiting independent human standards and assessment review.

privateAuthorNotes: A human reviewer must verify language-version accuracy, distractor validity, equivalence, and grading evidence before formal use.
items.title: Allocation and deallocation: reject the invalid rule
items.prompt: Which statement correctly resolves this C misconception? malloc initializes every allocated element to zero and realloc failure frees the original block automatically.
hints: Compare each option with the exact language rule, standard boundary, and observable evidence in the authored draft.

feedback.correct: Correct. malloc returns uninitialized storage and realloc failure leaves the original block allocated; check sizes and results before changing the owning pointer.
feedback.incorrect: That option preserves the documented misconception. malloc returns uninitialized storage and realloc failure leaves the original block allocated; check sizes and results before changing the owning pointer.

criteria.description: Selects the source-aligned C correction instead of the documented misconception.

options.text: malloc returns uninitialized storage and realloc failure leaves the original block allocated; check sizes and results before changing the owning pointer.
malloc initializes every allocated element to zero and realloc failure frees the original block automatically.

answer.explanation: malloc returns uninitialized storage and realloc failure leaves the original block allocated; check sizes and results before changing the owning pointer.

items.title: Allocation and deallocation: complete the language rule
items.prompt: Complete the missing C checkpoint in the source-aligned correction. Use the exact token or phrase required by this draft.
hints: Use the checkpoint that distinguishes the valid rule from this misconception: malloc initializes every allocated element to zero and realloc failure frees the original block automatically.

feedback.correct: Correct. The required checkpoint is 'uninitialized storage'.
feedback.incorrect: Recheck the exact language rule and its boundary. malloc returns uninitialized storage and realloc failure leaves the original block allocated; check sizes and results before changing the owning pointer.

criteria.description: Supplies the exact C token or phrase that completes the reviewed rule candidate.

items.template: malloc returns {{key-rule}} and realloc failure leaves the original block allocated; check sizes and results before changing the owning pointer.

answer.explanation: malloc returns uninitialized storage and realloc failure leaves the original block allocated; check sizes and results before changing the owning pointer.

items.title: Allocation and deallocation: executable application
items.prompt: Read a count from one to 100 followed by that many integers, allocate exactly the required C array with checked size and result, print their sum, and free the allocation on every completed path.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted deterministic draft awaiting independent human standards, security, and assessment review.

hints: Start from input validation and the declared observable output, then apply only the target language rule before considering optimization.

feedback.correct: All visible and hidden deterministic behavior checks passed for the bounded source task.
feedback.incorrect: At least one deterministic behavior check failed; inspect bounds, input state, and the target rule without exposing hidden cases.

criteria.description: Compiles in the pinned runtime and passes every normal and boundary behavior test.
Applies Allocation and deallocation without undefined behavior or an out-of-scope workaround.

privateAuthorNotes: AI-assisted and human-unreviewed: formal exam eligibility is prohibited until independent technical and assessment approval.
Reference verification uses the digest-pinned local container with network=none, no package installation, a read-only source mount, and deterministic stdin/stdout.
Executable facet: existing bounded executable application retained from the authored language tranche

answer.explanation: The reference solution exercises existing bounded executable application retained from the authored language tranche under the bounded deterministic contract; it remains an AI-assisted draft pending human review.
